revision.text:
'''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 - 7 February 622) was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japan royal family]] in the [[Asuka period]].She was the [[Empress of Japan|31th empress consort]], married to [[Emperor Yōmei]].
== Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe waa born into the ruling clan of Japan.She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane.The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers.One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]].
In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe she married her eldest half-brother, Imperial Prince Ōe, born by another consort of the emperor.Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins.The marriage produce four sons among which none of the ascended the throne.On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort (Chūgū) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension.
The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the cople had only one daughter.
* First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]])
== References ==
{{Consorts of Japan}}{{Japan-royal-stub}}

'''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 - 7 February 622) was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japan royal family]] in the [[Asuka period]].She was the [[Empress of Japan|31th empress consort]], married to [[Emperor Yōmei]].
== Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe waa born into the ruling clan of Japan.She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane.The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers.One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]].
In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe she married her eldest half-brother, Imperial Prince Ōe, born by another consort of the emperor.Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins.The marriage produce four sons among which none of the ascended the throne.On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort (Chūgū) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension.
The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the cople had only one daughter.
* First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]])
== References ==
{{Consorts of Japan}}{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]]

Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension.
The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the cople had only one daughter.

Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe waa born into the ruling clan of Japan.She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane.The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers.One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe she married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor.Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins.The marriage produce four sons among which none of the ascended the throne.On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the cople had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado (the Prince Shotoku) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a stable). == References == {{REFLIST}} {{Consorts of Japan}}{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]]

revision.text:
'''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 - 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧) was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japan royal family]] in the [[Asuka period]].She was the [[Empress of Japan|31th empress consort]], married to [[Emperor Yōmei]].{{Cite web |title=Princess Anahobe no Hashihito |url=https://artsandculture.google.com/entity/princess-anahobe-no-hashihito/g1218fk1l |access-date=2022-08-19 |website=Google Arts & Culture |language=en}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Empress Taiza
間人皇后 | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress of Japan|Empress Consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | house = [[Yamato period|Yamato]] | house-type = Clan | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka| Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |url-status=live |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m.564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the Temple [[Chūgū-ji]] for his mother.The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan.She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane.The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers.One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor.Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins.The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne.On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{REFLIST}} {{Consorts of Japan}}{{Japan-royal-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]]

revision.comment: rmv reference to clearly identified wikipedia mirror per [[WP:CIRCULAR]]

The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{REFLIST}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Daughters of emperors]] {{Japan-royal-stub}}

The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{REFLIST}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Daughters of emperors]] {{Japan-royal-stub}}

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]]

Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021年9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}{{citation needed|Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{citation needed|Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} {{citation needed|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} {{citation needed|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{citation needed|Anahobe no Hashihito, princess}} {{citation needed|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} {{citation needed|Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe}}

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
