{{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[Japan]] | burial_place = [[:en:Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[Ikaruga]], [[Yamato Province]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

She was the [[:en:Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]]

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[:en:Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[:en:Ikaruga|Ikaruga]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}}

It is said that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[:en:Ikaruga|Ikaruga]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}}

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[:en:Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[:en:Ikaruga|Ikaruga]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]][[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]][[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]][[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]][[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0560|death_date=0622; {{death date|0622|2|9}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Prince Tame|WD=Q11577211}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}}|children={{ill|Kume|WD=Q11523737}}; {{ill|Prince Ekuri|WD=Q11547041}}; {{ill|Prince Manda|WD=Q11617932}}; {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972}}; {{ill|Satomi no Himemiko|WD=Q11383115}}|father={{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}}|mother={{ill|Oane-no-kimi|WD=Q11625199}}|relatives={{ill|Bidatsu|WD=Q316637}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Amatsukabe Anahobe|WD=Q11597248}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami Be|WD=Q11584775}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Iso no Kami|WD=Q11584769}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11597250]] Wikidata name: Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] | succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] | father = [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | burial_place = [[:en:Eifuku-ji | Eifuku-ji Temple]], [[:en:Minamikawachi District, Osaka|Minamikawachi District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (m. 564)
Prince Tame | issue = [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]
Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[:en:Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[:en:Ikaruga|Ikaruga]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Japanese imperial family]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. She was the [[:en:Empress of Japan|empress consort]] through her marriage to [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. It is said that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the [[:en:Chūgū-ji|Chūgū-ji]] temple for his mother. The temple is located in [[:en:Ikaruga|Ikaruga]], [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become [[:en:Emperor Sushun|Emperor Sushun]]. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ([[:en:Kōkyū|Chūgū]]) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ([[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shotoku]]) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a [[:en:stable|stable]]). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Princess Nukatabe]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Empress Kōgyoku|Princess Takara]] | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese imperial family}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. She was the {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=empress consort}} through her marriage to {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}}. It is said that {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}, Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the {{ill|Chūgū-ji|en|Chūgū-ji|WD=Q1091232|lt=Chūgū-ji}} temple for his mother. The temple is located in {{ill|Ikaruga|en|Ikaruga|WD=Q1658068|lt=Ikaruga}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}}. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ({{ill|Kōkyū|en|Kōkyū|WD=Q13403578|lt=Chūgū}}) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor The temple is located in {{ill|Ikaruga|en|Ikaruga|WD=Q1658068|lt=Ikaruga}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}}. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ({{ill|Kōkyū|en|Kōkyū|WD=Q13403578|lt=Chūgū}}) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ({{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shotoku}}) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a {{ill|stable|en|stable|WD=Q214252|lt=stable}}). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Princess Takara}} | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese imperial family}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. She was the {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=empress consort}} through her marriage to {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}}. It is said that {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}, Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the {{ill|Chūgū-ji|en|Chūgū-ji|WD=Q1091232|lt=Chūgū-ji}} temple for his mother. The temple is located in {{ill|Ikaruga|en|Ikaruga|WD=Q1658068|lt=Ikaruga}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun}}. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ({{ill|Kōkyū|en|Kōkyū|WD=Q13403578|lt=Chūgū}}) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei}} (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ({{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shotoku}}) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a {{ill|stable|en|stable|WD=Q214252|lt=stable}}). == References == {{Reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Princess Takara}} | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan|state=collapsed}} [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[fa:شاهدخت هاشیهیتو نو آناهوبی]] [[ja:穴穂部間人皇女]] [[sv:Hashihito no Anahobe]] [[zh:穴穗部間人皇女]] {{wikidata link|Q11597250}} [[Category:560 births]] [[Category:622 deaths]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{ill|Imperial Prince Katsuraki|WD=Q11620913|1=Imperial Prince Katsuraki}}; {{ill|Imperial Prince Sakurawi|WD=Q11537858|1=Imperial Prince Sakurai}}; {{ill|Suiko|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}; {{ill|Sushun|WD=Q349238|1=Emperor Sushun}}; {{ill|Yōmei|WD=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}}}} {{Infobox royalty | consort = yes | name = Hashihito no Anahobe | image = Empress Hashiudo and Prince Shotoku Taishi monument ac (2).jpg | caption = Statue of Empress Taiza and her son, {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} | succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | reign = 1 January 585 – 21 May 587 | posthumous name = | royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | father = {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} | mother = Soga no Oane | birth_name = Hashihito no Anahobe | birth_date = 560 | birth_place = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | death_date = 621 (age 61) | death_place = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | burial_place = {{ill|Eifuku-ji |en|Eifuku-ji |WD=Q11410732|lt= Eifuku-ji Temple|1=Eifuku-ji}}, {{ill|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|en|Minamikawachi District, Osaka|WD=Q1347323|lt=Minamikawachi District|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{Cite web |title=Itineraries |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/modelcourse/course-minami-kawachi/ |access-date=13 August 2022 |website=OSAKAINFO}} | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (m. 564)

Prince Kume
Prince Eguri
Prince Mamuta
Princess Satomi| }} '''Princess Hashihito no Anahobe''' (穴穂部間人皇女, ''Anahobe no Hashihito no Himemiko,'' 560 – 7 February 622朝日日本歴史人物事典：（株）朝日新聞出版. “穴穂部間人皇女”. コトバンク. 2021st year 9月6日閲覧), also known as '''Empress Taiza''' (間人皇后), was a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Japanese imperial family|1=Imperial House of Japan}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. She was the {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=empress consort|1=Empress of Japan}} through her marriage to {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}}. It is said that {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}, Princess Anahobe's eldest son, built the {{ill|Chūgū-ji|en|Chūgū-ji|WD=Q1091232|lt=Chūgū-ji|1=Chūgū-ji}} temple for his mother. The temple is located in {{ill|Ikaruga|en|Ikaruga|WD=Q1658068|lt=Ikaruga|1=Ikaruga}}, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. [[File:Chūgū-ji temple , 中宮寺 - panoramio (4).jpg|thumb|Chūgū-ji temple]] == Genealogy == Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was born into the ruling clan of Japan. She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} and his consort, Soga no Oane. The princess has five full brothers: two elder brother and three younger brothers. One of her younger brother will later become {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|WD=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}. In 574, Princess Hashihito no Anahobe married her eldest half-brother, [[:en:Yōmei|Imperial Prince Ōe]], born by another consort of the emperor. Their mothers were full sisters, making them cousins. The marriage produce four sons among which none of them ascended the throne. On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ({{ill|Kōkyū|en|Kōkyū|WD=Q13403578|lt=Chūgū|1=Kōkyū}}) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ({{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shotoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a {{ill|stable|en|stable|WD=Q214252|lt=stable|1=Stable}}). == References == {{Reflist}} Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | after={{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|WD=Q235296|lt=Princess Takara|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} | years=586–587}} {{s-end}} On 3 October 585, prince Ōe became emperor and Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was named Empress Consort ({{ill|Kōkyū|en|Kōkyū|WD=Q13403578|lt=Chūgū|1=Kōkyū}}) in January of the same year. Sadly, her husband died three years later after his ascension. The widowed Empress married her stepson, Prince Tame, born by her husband's concubine, who also was her full sister.the couple had only one daughter. * First Husband: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|WD=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (用明天皇, 517 – 21 May 587), her half-brother and cousin ** Son: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} (聖徳太子, Shōtoku Taishi, 7 February 574 – 8 April 622) ** Son: Prince Kume (来目皇子, d.603) ** Son: Prince Eguri (殖栗皇子) ** Son: Prince Mamuta (茨田皇子, 579-643) * Second Husband: Prince Tame (田目皇子), her nephew and stepson ** Daughter: Princess Satōmi (佐富女王), Wife of Prince Hatsuse no Okimi (son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}) There is a famous anecdote in "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]" (Chronicles of Japan) that when Princess Hashihito no Anahobe was the empress of the Emperor Yōmei, she gave birth to the Prince Umayado ({{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shotoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}) at Umaya no toguchi (a door of a {{ill|stable|en|stable|WD=Q214252|lt=stable|1=Stable}}). == References == {{Reflist}}
